---
title: "Cultural Cognition, Environmental Orientation, and Pro-Environmental Behavior"
bibliography: references.bib
csl: american-political-science-association.csl
fig-cap-location: top
fontfamily: ebgaramond
link-bibliography: false
format: 
  pdf:
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \usepackage{dcolumn}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{amsmath}
        \addtokomafont{disposition}{\rmfamily}
  docx:
    reference-doc: sustainability-template.dot
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
# Clean, fit, and build all tables/figures (no side effects). Robust to cwd.
source(if (file.exists("scripts/analysis-prep.R")) "scripts/analysis-prep.R" else "../scripts/analysis-prep.R")
```

```{=html}
<!-- ===================== TABLES & FIGURES ======================
     Reference these in your prose with @tbl-... / @fig-... e.g.
     "as shown in @tbl-behaviors", "see @fig-cns and @fig-nep".
     Move the chunks wherever you want them to appear in the body.
     ============================================================= -->
```

<!-- Table 1 -->

```{r}
#| label: tbl-behaviors
#| tbl-cap: "Self-reported pro-environmental behaviors (means and standard deviations)"
knitr::kable(tbl1, booktabs = TRUE, align = c("l", "r", "r"))
```

<!-- Table 2 -->

```{r}
#| label: tbl-measurement
#| tbl-cap: "Measurement items and construct reliability (Cronbach's α, composite reliability, average variance extracted)"
knitr::kable(tbl2, booktabs = TRUE, align = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r"))
```

<!-- Figure 2 -->

```{r}
#| label: fig-cns
#| fig-cap: "Measurement and structural model of cultural cognition, the Connectedness to Nature Scale, and pro-environmental behaviors. Standardized coefficients shown; * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
draw_sem(fitCNS)
```

<!-- Figure 3 -->

```{r}
#| label: fig-nep
#| fig-cap: "Measurement and structural model of cultural cognition, the New Ecological Paradigm, and pro-environmental behaviors. Standardized coefficients shown; * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
draw_sem(fitNEP)
```

<!-- Table 3 -->

```{r}
#| label: tbl-fit
#| tbl-cap: "Model fit statistics for the CNS and NEP structural equation models"
knitr::kable(tbl3, booktabs = TRUE, align = c("l", rep("r", 8)))
```

<!-- Table 4 -->

```{r}
#| label: tbl-mediation
#| tbl-cap: "Mediation analysis: standardized direct, indirect, and total effects of cultural cognition on pro-environmental behavior († p<0.10, * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001)"
knitr::kable(tbl4, booktabs = TRUE, align = c("l", "r", "r", "r"))
```

\newpage

# Appendix

\setcounter{table}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{A\arabic{table}}

\newpage

# References

\setlength{\parindent}{-0.2in}
\setlength{\leftskip}{0.2in}
\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}
\vspace*{-0.2in}
\singlespace

\noindent
